subHeadline: The question every Mercedes-Benz owner eventually faces, answered honestly rather than sold around. We tell you when to repair, when to replace, and when to scrap — based on real UK data, not estimates.

verdict.text: Scrap threshold — recon cost frequently exceeds car value. The 70% Mercedes threshold is live here.
Safe — replacement well within value on both engine variants.
Watch — OM642 V6 recon on sub-£8k examples needs careful evaluation.
Safe — high-value SUV host; replacement comfortably viable.
Watch — flagship name but older values vary widely; assess per vehicle.
Safe — specialist-only, but AMG's UK network is well established and values absorb the cost.

ruleOfThumb.title: The Mercedes-Benz Rule of Thumb
ruleOfThumb.text: If reconditioned engine cost runs at 70% or more of what your car is actually worth on today's market, get a scrap valuation alongside any repair quote — don't decide on repair cost alone. This is our Mercedes-specific threshold, consistent with the same rule applied across the BMW site, reflecting the higher parts and labour baseline both premium German brands share.
